facts.text: McIntosh was officially incorporated in Florida as a municipality on May 22, 1913.
McIntosh’s name honors Colonel John Houstoun McIntosh, an early 1820s colonizer whose plantation was destroyed during the Second Seminole War.
McIntosh’s historic village showcases well‑preserved Victorian architecture and hosts the annual 1890s Festival, a living‑history event with period costumes and demonstrations.
The 1890s Festival draws visitors from across Central Florida each fall, featuring artisan vendors, food, and family‑friendly activities that transport guests back to Victorian times.
The town’s post office has operated continuously since 1887, making it one of the oldest in Marion County.
McIntosh lies about 20 miles north of Ocala, nestled in Marion County’s northern region, and is part of the Ocala Metropolitan Statistical Area.
